channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announces his resignation
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau has announced his resignation, telling the nation that “internal battles” mean he “cannot be the best option” in the next election. Trudeau said Monday he has asked the president of his Liberal Party to begin the process to select a new leader.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canada's Trudeau announces resignation after nearly a decade as prime minister
item.description: TORONTO (AP) — Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau announced his resignation Monday after nearly a decade in power, bowing to rising discontent over his leadership and growing turmoil within his government signaled by the abrupt departure of his finance minister.
item.dc:creator: ROB GILLIES, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US hoping for Gaza ceasefire agreement in next 2 weeks: Blinken
item.description: The Biden administration says it is still hoping to secure a ceasefire deal between Israel and Hamas in its final two weeks in the White House, Secretary of State Antony Blinken said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Musk says UK's Farage should not be Reform party leader
item.description: Tech billionaire Elon Musk said that British politician Nigel Farage should not be the Reform U.K. party’s leader.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated Maduro to meet Biden at White House
item.description: A senior U.S. administration official has told the Associated Press that the Venezuelan opposition leader who claims to have defeated President Nicolás Maduro in last year’s presidential election will meet with U.S. President Joe Biden at the White House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Regina Garcia Cano, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Pope Francis has named the first woman to head a major Vatican office
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis on Monday named the first woman to head a major Vatican office, appointing an Italian nun, Sister Simona Brambilla, to become prefect of the department responsible for all the Catholic Church's religious orders.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about the siege outside South Korea's presidential compound
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — A standoff between rival government forces outside the presidential compound in South Korea has been a startling development, even for observers used to the country's famously rough and tumble politics.
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German Christmas market attack victim dies at hospital weeks later, increasing death toll to 6
item.description: A woman who was injured during the Dec. 20 attack at a German Christmas market died at a hospital on Monday, increasing the death toll to six.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Russia claims it captured another town in eastern Ukraine but Kyiv's troops say the fight continues
item.description: Russia’s Defense Ministry is claiming that its troops in eastern Ukraine have captured the town of Kurakhove after a monthslong battle.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: South Korea says North Korea fired a ballistic missile into the sea
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea on Monday fired a ballistic missile that flew 1,100 kilometers (685 miles) before landing in waters between the Korean Peninsula and Japan, South Korea’s military said, extending its weapons testing weeks before Donald Trump returns as U.S. president.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: France's former President Sarkozy stands trial over alleged campaign funding by Libya's Gadhafi
item.description: A trial has opened of France’s former President Nicolas Sarkozy and 11 co-defendants over alleged illegal financing of his 2007 presidential campaign by the government of then-Libyan leader Moammar Gadhafi.
item.dc:creator: Nicolas Vaux-Montagny And Sylvie Corbet, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: More than 30 dolphins have died since an oil spill near southern Russia, experts say
item.description: Thirty-two dolphins have died since fuel oil spilled out of two storm-stricken tankers three weeks ago in the Kerch Strait, which separates the Russia-occupied Crimean Peninsula from Russia’s southern Krasnodar region, an animal rescue group said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Thousands protest in Montenegro to demand ouster of top security officials over mass shooting
item.description: PODGORICA, Montenegro (AP) — Several thousand people rallied in Montenegro on Sunday demanding the resignations of top security officials over the shooting earlier this week that left 12 people dead, including two children.
item.dc:creator: PREDRAG MILIC, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: China fires shot across Trump's bow with defense sanctions
item.description: China’s decision this week to slap several major U.S. defense firms with penalizing trade measures is being viewed as a “shot across the bow” ahead of President-elect Trump’s inauguration. The move — targeting defense contractors Raytheon, Boeing, General Dynamics and Lockheed Martin — adds to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, The Hill

item.title: Austrian nationalist party leader rumored to be in talks to form government
item.description: Herbert Kickl, leader of Austria's right-wing Freedom Party, met with President Alexander Van der Bellen on Sunday, further fueling rumors that he will be asked to form a government.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli PM office denies reports that Hamas forwarded list of hostages to release in event of deal
item.description: Israel denied reports that Hamas forwarded a list of 34 hostages it could release in exchange for a cease-fire Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korean protesters brave cold to demand Yoon's ouster as detention deadline looms
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — Hundreds of South Koreans, bundled up against freezing temperatures and snow, rallied overnight into Sunday near the residence of impeached President Yoon Suk Yeol, calling for his ouster and arrest, as authorities prepared to renew their efforts to detain him over his shor
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israeli airstrikes in Gaza kill at least 21, hospital workers say, as talks underway
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli airstrikes killed at least 21 people, including at least two children, on Saturday in Gaza, hospital staff said, while a new effort at ceasefire talks was underway in Qatar after nearly 15 months of war.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: World’s oldest person dies in Japan at 116
item.description: Tomiko Itooka, the oldest person in the world, has died at 116. She was born in Osaka, Japan, in 1908 and is survived by one son, one daughter and five grandchildren.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Central American troops arrive in Haiti to reinforce mission to restore order
item.description: PORT-AU-PRINCE, Haiti (AP) — About 150 military police officers from Central America have arrived in Haiti to reinforce the embattled government's fight against violent gangs that have upended daily life for millions in the Caribbean country.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Royal family pays tribute to Prince William, Harry's nanny who lost stepson in New Orleans attack
item.description: Prince William shared his condolences to the family of his former nanny after her stepson was killed in the New Year’s terror attack in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, The Hill

item.title: Bipartisan lawmakers renew push for legislation supporting Georgia against Russian interference
item.description: Republican and Democratic lawmakers are renewing efforts to pass legislation supporting Georgia, touting it as one of the best and first examples of bipartisan unity of the new Congress. Reps. Joe Wilson (R-S.C.) and Steve Cohen (D-Tenn.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: One cigarette can decrease life expectancy by 20 minutes: Data
item.description: In 2000, data published in The BMJ estimated one cigarette would cost a person 11 minutes of their life.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Zelenskyy says Trump could be ‘decisive’ in bringing an end to the war
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said he thinks President-elect Donald Trump could be "decisive" in ending the nearly three-years-long war and dealing with Russian President Vladimir Putin's demands.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 30 people killed by Israeli strikes in Gaza
item.description: At least 30 people, including children, were killed in Gaza by Israeli strikes overnight and into Friday morning.
item.dc:creator: WAFAA SHURAFA and SAM MEDNICK, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Iran executes over 1K prisoners in 2024, highest total in 30 years, report says
item.description: A new report claimed Iran executed an unprecedented number of people in 2024. Most of the executions took place following the election of its new president in late July.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: ISIS increasingly unopposed following US withdrawal from Afghanistan, collapse of Syria
item.description: The Islamic State is seeing less resistance in areas of major security concern following the U.S. withdrawal from Afghanistan, the fall of the Assad regime in Syria and potential troop drawdown in Iraq.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Zealand police officer killed in New Year's Day vehicle attack, another injured
item.description: A driver ran down two New Zealand police officers on patrol in the early hours of New Year’s Day, killing one and seriously injuring the other, the country’s police chief said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: German chancellor takes veiled swipe at Elon Musk in New Year's address
item.description: Scholz's comments come days after the German government accused the billionaire of trying to influence its February election.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, WGN 9

item.title: The world welcomes 2025 with light shows, embraces and ice plunges
item.description: The New Year’s Eve ball dropped in soggy Times Square, where thousands of revelers stuck it out in heavy rain to celebrate.
item.dc:creator: MELISSA GOLDIN, CEDAR ATTANASIO and CHARLOTTE GRAHAM-McLAY, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel kills Hamas commander who led heinous Oct. 7 attack on Kibbutz Nir Oz killed in drone attack: IDF
item.description: The Israel Defense Force announced that they had successfully killed one of the terrorist leaders who was responsible for the Oct. 7 attack on Israel in a targeted attack.
item.dc:creator: Fox News
